column1: Modes in this game have a dmg type debuff and attribute restrictions for access, so trying to build a "neutral" party will lead to you being barred from playing these said modes.
column7:
Newbie Tips:
1. No single team clears all content—you need multiple parties.
2. Don’t focus only on dupes of favorites; learn the game first.
3. Only unlock units (T) you’ll build soon — avoid waste.
4. Get every unit possible from SR Tickets whenever you obtain one.

column6:

Because of [Assembly], it is not advised to invest in Rufus
column10:
Should be who you select as your first SR Hero
She is the best DPS starting out & just works.
[It doesn't matter who you get. Yes, you get enough resources obtain every single hero in the game. So do so]

column7:
Silver Knight's Special Training! unlocks after completing World 1.
It's a progression questline rewarding multiple growth material.
It's done naturally as you play & don't need to be hard focused on.
Essence
The missions do get gradually harder as you progress. Just keep grinding & pushing.

When you're able to, spend your gems on expanding your Research & Expedition Slots. Using your Gold, expand all of your inventories!
This will help level up your party skills & passive stats quicker, brings in more rewards while AFK, & lets you hold more items.

column9: Use Fragments & Blessings of Valor to level up stats for each of the five classes.

column7: Fragments
This is your first direct BP boost. Level all classes evenly with Fragments, then use Blessings to max Mages and Rangers first, followed by Assaults. Tanks and Healers can wait.
Maxing Hero Training (HT) is a time-gated grind. Lol. Have fun.

column1: Support Party: You Made It To World 3 !

Upon reaching World 3, you unlock the "SUPPORT PARTY" feature, allowing you to borrow 2 fully invested SR units for story mode. This speeds up PvE, & with just your own Tank & Healer, you can realistically clear the story.
column9: These two will carry you through Worlds 3 – 9. Maybe even 10. Meanwhile, start building Ai (T) or Callisto (T) to prepare for the steep difficulty spike in World 10 or 11 & beyond when attributes become important.

column3:
The two dailies reward you with Gold.
Daily Boss also rewards you with accessories that can be fused for a chance to obtain Tiered Accessories
column8: Gold is used for quite literally everything. For Daily Bosses, if your stock allows it, attack Gardosen daily. On Saturday & Sunday, kill Mercuria, as she offers the most gold. Weekends have a Daily Bonus of 50% more gold from Daily Boss.

column3:
Defense is your daily source of awakening cubes & chaser crystals.
Annihilation gives Chaser Crystal & Soul Imprint Cubes.
You need Chaser Crystals for Soul Essence, so these two modes go hand in hand during character progression.
column10: It's rec'd to Gem annihilation daily, if your income allows it, as Growth Cubes are essential. Essence isn't too much of a gatekeep the more you play, but you may always need Cubes

PvP = DAILY: Gives you Yellow Gems, which summon Growth Cubes for a specific hero; you need to do at least 5 PvP matches a day to capitalise off of this
Arena = WEEKLY: Gives everything accessory related. Tiered Accessories are mainly grinded here.

column3: Three different mob related stages. This mode is where you grind stones to either transcend your Main Weapon or promote your slots. This mode is highly attribute racist but can be done [even if just barely], with varying results, with either mages or rangers, excluding part 1
column9:
The stones are used for promoting slots & transcending weapons. Trust when you're told the RNG for slots is horrible. You will be farming forever & you will love it
[Invest In Hammers]

column2: Stages that spawn the most random heroes for you to take down. Rewards you with artifacts & artifacts shields. Farming this goes hand in hand with the Exchange Market, being a nice gold farming simulator

column3: Every so often, more times than probably needed, the game has events & battlepasses that will reward you with... a bit of everything. Being active while they are around can be beneficial for you. Don't be enticed to spend, though. Unless...
column7: BP Guide

column1: There is a questline for equipment when you reach a certain point in the game. The equipment they give you can be skipped entirely in favor of crafting the highest tier [or 2nd highest] of equipment available

column2: Raid will give you material to craft weapons & armor. It's rec'd to craft the highest you can at first, skipping the level penalty. This can be done by doing BASIC Dual Raid, letting other players carry you. Just bring a healer that regens SP & a good tank!
column7: As for as doing raid goes, you can bring support units required for Raid & ask for help online! Being carried is a plus for both players. If you cannot do the latest Raid on release, or upon you starting, don't worry. Basic Raid is still better than running Solo Raid. Get carried!

column1:
All equips have additional stats & enchantable substats.
You can set a weapon with maxed crit chance on one preset & one with max cooldown reduction on another for the same class, equipping them to heroes that require whatever substat over the other. This means that you generally want multiple sets for each class based on different stats/enchants. Presets can be shared for multiple characters of the same class.
column7:
Additional Stats: Require material from Dual Raid to reroll
Enchantable Substats: Can be acquired from DB & Dungeons
[Don't buy them from the Guild Shop]

Recommended to Reset Raid at least once daily & Chasm at least once weekly.
Even after you finish crafting equipment, upgrading slots will have you down bad horrendously without doing so.
